text: The surgeon begins the procedure by dividing the lesser omentum, revealing the caudate lobe of the liver. As the omental tissue is divided upwards, the right crus of the diaphragm becomes visible, and the area around the crus is cleared.
The surgeon places a holding suture in the surgical field, securing tissue in preparation for further manipulation.

The surgeon places a traction suture over the xiphoid process using a straight long needle, pulling the suture through the crux of the diaphragm and passing it outside, just beneath the costal margin.
The surgeon uses a switch to retract the liver upwards, providing a clear view of the surgical field.
